title: pimelea aridula subsp. oliga
intro: Pimelea aridula subsp. oliga is a rare and endemic subspecies of shrub native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Thymelaeaceae. The plant is also known as the 'Fairy duster' or 'Fairy bush' due to its small, white flowers resembling a duster or brush. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and protecting against cellular damage. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas and infusions, which are believed to promote digestive health and alleviate respiratory issues.
